releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
Non-blocking warning cleanup from the automated review. No user-facing behavior changes.

- Deletions now use `FileManager.trashFile` (respects your trash preference) instead of `Vault.delete`/`Vault.trash`.
- Dropped the `builtin-modules` build dependency in favor of Node's `module.builtinModules`.
- Removed unused local variables.
releases.name: 0.13.4 — community-review warning cleanup

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
Clears the blocking errors from the Obsidian Community Plugins automated review. No user-facing behavior changes.

- **No inline styles** — every `el.style.x = …` is now `el.setCssStyles({ … })`.
- **minAppVersion → 1.11.4** — matches the SecretStorage + `revealLeaf` APIs in use.
- **No plugin-as-component** — `MarkdownRenderer.render` receives the view, not the plugin instance.
- **Popout-window compatibility** — `window.*` timers + `activeDocument`.
- Minor: described eslint-disable directive, removed unused imports, `vault.configDir` in the settings warning.
releases.name: 0.13.3 — community-review compliance fixes

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
Fixes from the Obsidian Community Plugins automated review.

- **Source-available** — the repository now ships the full TypeScript source (`src/`, `defaults/`, build config), so the directory's source-code check passes.
- **Manifest description** no longer contains the word "Obsidian".

No functional changes vs 0.13.1 — `main.js` is byte-identical.
releases.name: 0.13.2 — community-directory submission fixes

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
Pre-submission compliance polish for the Obsidian Community Plugins directory. No functional changes.

- Conversations-panel toggle icon built with the DOM API instead of an HTML string (icon unchanged).
- `onunload` no longer detaches plugin leaves (Obsidian restores view positions on update).
- Command names are sentence case and no longer repeat the plugin name; command IDs unchanged, so hotkeys keep working.
- Settings-tab headings use `setHeading()`; removed the redundant title.
- Removed stray `console.log` output.

> First release tagged without a `v` prefix (tag `0.13.1` == manifest version) so it satisfies the community-directory submission check.
releases.name: 0.13.1 — community-plugin compliance polish

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
**MCP v2** — register an MCP server **once** in Agent Fleet and it's available to **any** agent on **either** adapter (Claude Code or Codex).

- **One registry, projected per run.** Servers live under `_fleet/mcp/<name>.md` and are projected into each run — Claude via `--mcp-config`, Codex via `-c mcp_servers.*`. Native `~/.claude.json` and `~/.codex/config.toml` are read-only; Agent Fleet never mutates them.
- **Cross-adapter auth.** OAuth 2.1 (or a static bearer token) authenticated once, stored in the OS keychain, projected to both backends (Claude header / Codex `bearer_token_env_var`).
- **One-time import** of your existing Claude + Codex servers (deduplicated, tokens moved to the keychain).
- **Robustness.** Retired the brittle `claude mcp list` / `codex mcp list` text-parsing; a bad server is dropped without breaking the run.

See the [changelog](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for details.
releases.name: v0.13.0 — MCP v2: register once, works on any adapter

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
Bug-fix release for the v0.12.0 memory subsystem — chiefly channel agents going silent.

## Channel agents stopped replying
- **Empty cwd:** the default `cwd: ""` didn't fall back to the vault base (`??` doesn't catch an empty string), so v0.12.0's `remember` tool built invalid temp paths and the spawn failed. Now empty cwd falls back to the vault base, and the remember-tool install is fail-soft (never breaks a reply).
- **Expired Claude session:** channel agents reconnect via `claude --resume <id>`; when that session was pruned/expired, Claude returned nothing and the dead id was never dropped, so the agent stayed silent forever. Now a resumed turn that errors or comes back empty clears the id and the next turn starts fresh (matching the Codex path).

## /agents picker showed phantom agents
The Slack/Telegram `/agents` picker and `@`-prefix routing listed `allowed_agents` verbatim, so a stale/renamed/disabled entry appeared as selectable and failed on use. The list is now intersected with the agents that actually exist and are enabled.

## Other
Memory capture hygiene: pending-file drain removes before folding (no duplicate raw entries on transient failure); reflection persists its candidate ledger per proposal; removed dead code.

Requires Obsidian 1.6.0+. Tests: 210 passing.
releases.name: v0.12.1 — channel reply fixes + /agents picker validation

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
A full rewrite of agent memory, plus capture parity for Codex and chat-UI polish.

## Memory subsystem v2
Memory moves from a single flat file to a per-agent folder: a curated, **token-budgeted `working.md`** injected into every run, plus an append-only **`raw/`** ground-truth log so summaries can always be re-derived. Legacy files migrate automatically (seeding the raw archive first).

- **Capture on every surface** (task, heartbeat, chat) on **both Claude Code and OpenAI Codex** — via a dedicated **`remember` tool** (preferred) or the **`[REMEMBER]`** text tag (fallback). Captures are serialized through a per-agent lock, sanitized, and deduped.
- **Reflection ("dreaming")** — optional nightly run that consolidates memory from the raw log (dedup, resolve contradictions, summarize-from-ground-truth to fit the budget, never drop pinned preferences). With `reflection_propose_skills`, recurring friction becomes an **approval-gated skill proposal** in the Inbox.
- `memory_token_budget` replaces the old (unenforced) `memory_max_entries`.

## Chat UI
- Conversations fold/unfold toggle now uses Obsidian's native sidebar-toggle look (backgroundless, animated, exact geometry).
- Conversations panel header aligned with the panel typography.

Built on top of v0.11.0 (OpenAI Codex backend). Requires Obsidian 1.6.0+. Tests: 210 passing.

See the [Agent Memory section of the README](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet#agent-memory).
releases.name: v0.12.0 — Memory subsystem v2 (two-tier store, reflection, skill proposals)

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
Agents can now run on **OpenAI Codex** as an alternative to Claude Code, chosen per agent — chat, tasks, heartbeat, Slack/Telegram channels, memory, and the dashboard all work identically on either backend.

## Highlights

- **OpenAI Codex CLI backend** — set an agent's *Adapter* to `OpenAI Codex` to run it on `codex exec` instead of Claude Code. Mix Claude and Codex agents freely in one fleet. Requires `@openai/codex` (ChatGPT plan or OpenAI API key). The Codex CLI path is only probed when an agent actually uses it.
- **Permission rules on Codex** — `Bash(...)` allow/deny rules are now enforced on Codex too, translated to execpolicy command rules and injected via a per-agent `CODEX_HOME` overlay. Every failure path is soft: missing execpolicy support, no `~/.codex`, or unavailable symlinks all fall back to sandbox-only enforcement with a warning.
- **Model picker** switches its alias list to match the selected adapter (Claude aliases vs Codex slugs); free text remains the escape hatch.

## Notes & limits

- Codex enforcement is lossy by design: only command-prefix `Bash(...)` patterns translate. Tool-name rules (`Read`/`Write`/`Edit`) and mid-pattern wildcards are dropped — file/network access is governed by **Permission Mode** (the sandbox). There's no closed allow-list on Codex (`allow` rules are additive).
- Codex runs report no dollar cost, so `cost_usd` is blank on those runs; compact/rate-limit telemetry stays Claude-only.

See the [Backends section of the README](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet#backends) and the [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet-private) for full detail.

**Tests:** 161/161 green.
releases.name: v0.11.0 — OpenAI Codex CLI backend + execpolicy permissions

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## 0.10.3 — 2026-05-11

One feature, one fix. Both surfaced by real usage.

### Parallel in-app conversations per agent

Hold N independent chats with the same agent in the Obsidian chat panel — different tasks, different contexts, switchable from a dropdown. Reuses the conversation plumbing that already powers Slack multi-thread.

- Same-agent dedup is now `(agent, conversationId)` dedup. Two tabs on the same agent are fine; two tabs on the same `(agent, conversation)` reveals the existing tab.
- A conversation picker row appears below the agent dropdown whenever the agent has more than one conversation. Single-conversation users see no UI change.
- **+ New Chat** creates a new conversation alongside the current one (was: wipe-the-singleton). Rename / Delete buttons next to the picker; the legacy "Main chat" is delete-protected so pre-feature data can't be orphaned accidentally.
- Tab title becomes `Chat: <agent> · <conversation name>` once a second conversation exists.

**Data layout** mirrors Slack sessions:
- Folder agents: `<agent-folder>/conversations/<convId>.json`
- Flat agents: `_fleet/memory/<agent>-conversations/<convId>.json`
- Sentinel id `"default"` maps back to the legacy per-agent `chat.json` so existing data is untouched. The picker surfaces it as "Main chat".

**State persistence**: `AgentChatView.getState()` now returns `{agentName, conversationId}`. Reload restores the exact same tab → same conversation. Older state files without `conversationId` fall back to `"default"`.

**Engine unchanged**: `ChatSessionOptions.inAppConversationId` joins existing `channelName` / `conversationId` / `threadAnchorId` knobs; `getChatFilePath()` grows one parallel branch. The Claude `--resume` flow, sendMessage lock, watchdog, threading, and stats all carry through.

### Fix: stop old runtime's crons on saveSettings rebuild

`saveSettings()` was replacing `this.runtime` with a fresh `FleetRuntime` without stopping the outgoing runtime's heartbeat / task-scheduler crons. Croner timers own their own callbacks once started — dropping the reference doesn't stop them, so each leaked runtime kept firing the schedules it was registered with for the rest of the Obsidian session.

Concrete symptom: a user changes an agent's heartbeat from "every 2h" to "every 6h", and the 2h schedule keeps firing. Even though the agent-edit path correctly re-registered on the current runtime, any prior `saveSettings()` (toggling status bar, editing channel credentials, etc.) had leaked a previous runtime still holding a 2h cron. Multiple leaked runtimes accumulated across a session, causing duplicate runs and making schedule edits appear not to take effect until full reload.

- `TaskScheduler.shutdown()` — stop every job, clear the map, drop the pending queue.
- `FleetRuntime.shutdown()` — stop heartbeat crons + delegate to scheduler. Idempotent.
- `main.saveSettings()` calls `runtime.shutdown()` before reassigning.
- `main.onunload()` calls `runtime.shutdown()` so "Disable plugin" without a full reload also tears down cleanly.

Regression test added on `TaskScheduler` — verifies post-shutdown `resumeAll` doesn't flush queued runs and no cron remains to fire.

### Tests

108/108 green (up from 103): 4 new conversation-path tests on ChatSession + 1 scheduler-shutdown regression.

See [v0.10.0](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet/releases/tag/v0.10.0) for the major Wiki Keeper / permissions release this patches.
releases.name: v0.10.3 — parallel in-app conversations + cron-leak fix

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## 0.10.2 — 2026-05-08

Two limits hit by busy fleets are now configurable / accurate.

### Configurable chat watchdog timeout

The chat-session watchdog (kills the Claude CLI subprocess if no stream events arrive mid-turn) was hardcoded to 5 minutes — too short for agents running long tools (large web fetches, builds, big greps), causing spurious "no response from the CLI" errors.

Now configurable via `chatWatchdogMinutes` in plugin settings (default 10 min, range 1–60). `ChatSession` reads the value at every watchdog arm, so changes apply on the next turn without recreating sessions.

`ExecutionManager` keeps using the per-agent `agent.timeout` for task / heartbeat hard-cap — that's a different concern (wall-clock cap on an entire run) and intentionally untouched.

### Dashboard 14-day chart now accurate on busy fleets

The Run Activity bar chart was being fed by `runtime.getRecentRuns()`, which is capped at 50 runs. On a fleet with frequent heartbeats / scheduled tasks, those 50 runs only spanned a few days — older bars in the 14-day window appeared empty even when they had real activity.

Fix: new `FleetRepository.listRunsSince(date)` walks `_fleet/runs/YYYY-MM-DD/` folders lexicographically `>= sinceDate`. Cost is bounded by window size, not total historical runs. New parallel `chartRuns` cache populated alongside `recentRuns` via a new `refreshRunCaches()` helper used by all 7 refresh sites. Bar chart uses the new list; success-rate donut and activity timeline keep the recent-50 list (those are "lately" indicators, not 14-day windows).

103/103 tests green. No schema or default-content changes.

See [v0.10.0](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet/releases/tag/v0.10.0) for the major feature release this patches.
releases.name: v0.10.2 — configurable chat watchdog + 14-day chart accuracy

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## 0.10.1 — 2026-04-26

**Fix**: Wiki Keeper heartbeat/task runs were hitting Claude Code v2.1.x's spawned-subprocess Bash sandbox, blocking `Bash(mv ...)` and causing the model to fall back to Write — which copies a file to archive but doesn't delete the source. Result: inbox files reprocessed every cycle.

`ExecutionManager` now passes `--permission-mode <mode>` to the spawned Claude CLI explicitly (matching `ChatSession`'s behavior, which is why interactive chat already worked). Without the explicit CLI flag, Claude Code's `defaultMode` in `settings.local.json` was insufficient to opt out of sandbox for non-interactive subprocesses.

No changes to skill prompts, defaults, or schemas.

See [v0.10.0](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet/releases/tag/v0.10.0) for the major release this patches.
releases.name: v0.10.1 — fix heartbeat/task sandbox blocking inbox→archive moves

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
# Changelog

## 0.10.0 — 2026-04-26

Wiki Keeper grows from a nightly batch ingester into a self-maintaining knowledge base in the Karpathy "LLM wiki" sense — and the underlying permission system is rebuilt so UI edits actually reach the spawned Claude process.

### Wiki Keeper — compounding loop

- **New skill: `wiki-refresh`.** Regenerates a stable, fenced `## Summary` block at the top of every topic page whose claim history has grown. Bounded by its own `max_tokens_per_refresh` budget. Idempotent.
- **End-of-ingest refresh phase.** `wiki-ingest` tracks every page it touched and chains `wiki-refresh` at the end of each run. Topic pages stop growing into 200-line changelogs without a synthesis on top.
- **Q&A compounding (Karpathy's central pattern).** `file_substantive_answers` now defaults to **on**. Substantive `wiki-query` answers file as both a synthesis page AND dated bullets on each cited topic page. Every meaningful question leaves a per-topic trace, not just a sidecar synthesis.
- **Provenance footer on every answer.** Citations carry the `summary_refreshed`/mtime of each cited page so readers see staleness.

### Wiki Keeper — integrity

- **Bidirectional cross-link check** in `wiki-lint`: flags edges where A→B exists in summary/prose but B has no mention of A.
- **Dedup proposals**: title-similarity (Levenshtein) + co-citation overlap; never auto-merges.
- **Stale-summary detection** in lint: pages whose `summary_refreshed` is missing or older than `summary_stale_days` get chained to `wiki-refresh` automatically.
- **Hash-based watched-mode idempotency**: SHA-1 of normalized content. Skips re-processing files whose mtime moved without content change (defends against iCloud / Obsidian Sync touch-without-edit).
- **Failed-source quarantine**: inbox files that fail ingest 3 times in a row move to `_sources/failed/` with a sidecar `.error.md` instead of being retried forever.
- **Source-type tags** on every dated bullet: `[doc]`, `[meeting]`, `[email]`, `[note]`, `[web]`, `[synthesis]`, `[other]`. Helps query weight evidence quality.
- **Index sub-MOC awareness**: when topics root exceeds `index_split_threshold` (default 100) or any single type exceeds 30 pages, ingest writes per-type sub-MOCs instead of one flat alphabetical list.
- **Syntheses exempt from orphan flagging** if they have outbound links to topic pages they cite.

### Wiki Keeper — dashboard

- **New "Wiki Keepers" main nav page**. Per-instance card showing scope, log shortcut, and the latest `## Lint YYYY-MM-DD` block parsed from `log.md`. Needs review items render as actionable cards with Dismiss.
- Settings → Wiki Keeper edit form now exposes `max_tokens_per_refresh`, `index_split_threshold`, `summary_stale_days`, and `dedup_similarity_threshold`.

### Wiki Keeper — schema additions

Topic-page frontmatter gains `summary_refreshed: <ISO>` and `claims_at_refresh: <int>`. The auto-managed summary block lives between `<!-- wiki-keeper:summary:begin -->` and `<!-- wiki-keeper:summary:end -->` markers — `wiki-refresh` is the only writer; `## Claims` history and user-authored prose elsewhere on the page are untouched.

### Permission management — full rebuild

The permission system had silent drift: UI edits wrote `allowed_tools`/`blocked_tools` to `config.md` frontmatter, but the runtime ignored those fields and read `permissions.json` exclusively. Wiki Keeper Edit → Save was effectively a no-op for permissions, and ChatSessions silently dropped allow/deny rules entirely.

- **Single source of truth.** `permissions.json` is the canonical surface for allow/deny on folder agents. Flat agents get a `<name>.permissions.json` sidecar — they previously had no allow/deny support at all.
- **Vestigial fields removed.** `agent.allowedTools` / `agent.blockedTools` deleted from the type. `config.md` no longer carries `allowed_tools` / `blocked_tools`. Migration on read picks up legacy values; saving once rewrites them to the canonical surface.
- **Wiki Keeper canonical permissions.** New `ensureWikiKeeperPermissions` helper merges the canonical allow (`Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash(mv *), Bash(mkdir *)`) and canonical deny (including defense-in-depth path-traversal patterns: `Bash(rm -rf /*)`, `Bash(mv * /*)`, `Bash(cp -r * /*)`) into `permissions.json`. Idempotent — preserves user-added entries. Both `createWikiKeeperAgent` and `updateWikiKeeperAgent` call it, so Edit → Save now actually upgrades older keepers.
- **Permissions apply in chat sessions.** Previously chat sessions only honored `--permission-mode` and dropped `permissionRules.allow/deny` — meaning a Wiki Keeper chatting interactively could not run its allow-listed `Bash(mv *)` without per-call approval. `ChatSession` now writes `.claude/settings.local.json` at process spawn (and restores at close / abort / hibernate / error), via a shared `claudeSettings` util reused by `ExecutionManager`.
- **Long-lived sessions resolve fresh.** `ChatSession.refreshAgent()` re-resolves `repository.getAgentByName(name)` at every process spawn. Permission/model/effort edits made in the UI take effect on the next message in the same chat tab — no need to recreate it. Falls back to construction-time agent if the agent has been deleted.
- **No race between Save and Run.** All Wiki Keeper modal handlers `await plugin.refreshFromVault()` before re-rendering. The 500ms vault-watcher debounce no longer matters.

### Federator (design only)

- New `WIKI_KEEPER_FEDERATOR_DESIGN.md` specifies a v2 cross-scope orchestrator: a single agent that fans out to multiple keepers, merges cited answers with explicit per-scope provenance, and routes inbox drops to the right keeper. No code in this release.

### Tests

- 12 new tests covering the permission lifecycle, lint report parsing, and `wiki-refresh` synthesis schema (103 / 103 green).
- `claudeSettings` util has dedicated coverage for the settings.local.json write/restore lifecycle.
- `ensureWikiKeeperPermissions` covered for fresh write, additive merge, idempotency, invalid-JSON recovery.
- `ChatSession.refreshAgent` covered for fresh-fetch and fallback-on-deletion.

### Files of note

- New: `defaults/skills/wiki-refresh/skill.md`, `src/utils/claudeSettings.ts`, `src/utils/wikiLintReport.ts`, `WIKI_KEEPER_FEDERATOR_DESIGN.md`
- Significantly rewritten: `defaults/skills/wiki-ingest/skill.md`, `defaults/skills/wiki-query/skill.md`, `defaults/skills/wiki-lint/skill.md`, `src/wikiKeeperTemplate.ts`
- Touched: `src/types.ts`, `src/fleetRepository.ts`, `src/services/chatSession.ts`, `src/services/executionManager.ts`, `src/settingsTab.ts`, `src/views/dashboardView.ts`

### Migration notes

- Existing Wiki Keepers: open Settings → Wiki Keepers → ⚙ Edit → Save once. Strips dead `allowed_tools` / `blocked_tools` from `config.md` and merges canonical allow/deny into `permissions.json` (preserving any custom entries).
- Existing topic pages: keep working as-is. The first `wiki-ingest` after upgrade adds the fenced summary block and frontmatter slots to pages it touches.
- Existing flat agents with `allowed_tools` / `blocked_tools` in frontmatter: unchanged behaviorally on read (legacy migration), but a Save through the UI will move the rules into a sidecar `<name>.permissions.json` and strip the frontmatter fields.
releases.name: v0.10.0 — Wiki Keeper compounding loop + permission management rebuild

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## Highlights

### Wiki Keeper
- **Scheduling consolidated**: removed `ingest_schedule`/`lint_schedule` from `config.md`. Cadence is now owned by `HEARTBEAT.md` (for ingest) and the sibling `<agent>-lint` task (for lint). No more double-fire when both were set.
- **`watched_since` date cutoff**: established vaults no longer get flooded on first run. Defaults to today at creation; clear to process all history.
- **Clearer watched-mode guidance**: `wiki-ingest` skill now has explicit anti-patterns (no per-source dump pages, no catch-all concept page). Agents correctly distribute claims across per-subject topic pages.
- **UI parity with config**: Add/Edit Wiki Keeper modals now expose all six paths (`inboxPath`, `archivePath`, `topicsRoot`, `indexPath`, `logPath`, `stateFile`) plus `fileSubstantiveAnswers` and `obsidianUrlScheme` toggles.

### Auto-compact
- Post-compact stats now snap to the real `post_tokens` value from the CLI, so the context bar drops to the correct percentage immediately.
- Fixed a bug where stale pre-compact usage could re-trigger a second compact on a freshly-compacted context.
- Compact is now a muted inline notice in the stats strip (self-clears on next user turn), no longer an error-style chat bubble.

### Form gap closures
- **Task form**: new Recurring/One-time mode selector with a datetime picker for `run_at`. Once-type tasks are now reachable from the UI.
- **Channel form**: tags, markdown body, and transport JSON editor (Advanced section).
- **Wiki Keeper modals**: path overrides + the two toggles mentioned above.

### Threads
- Thread composer now supports the **+ button** (attach active note) and **image paste / drag-drop**, matching the main chat. Each open thread has independent attachment state.

## Install / upgrade

- **BRAT users**: "Check for updates" — v0.9.2 will be picked up automatically.
- **Manual install**: download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` below into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/agent-fleet/` and reload Obsidian.
- **npm users**: `npm install -g obsidian-agent-fleet@0.9.2` (after this package is published — see note below if pulling at release time).

## Known followups

Five agent-form fields (`allowedTools`, `blockedTools`, `maxRetries`, `memoryMaxEntries`, `env`) are parsed from config.md but currently not consumed by the runtime. They'll be wired up or removed in v0.9.3 — no user action needed.
releases.name: v0.9.2 — Wiki Keeper polish, compact UX, form gap closures, thread attachments

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
A patch release on top of [v0.9.0](../v0.9.0) focused on robustness, stuck-state recovery, and exposing previously-hidden config.

## Highlights

### 🛟 Error surfacing + stuck-state recovery
- CLI error events (API failures, context overflows, rate limits) now render a red error bubble in chat instead of silently hanging. `isStreaming` is guaranteed to clean up so the stop button never permanently sticks.
- **5-minute watchdog** guards against silent CLI hangs. If no stream events arrive while streaming, the turn is auto-rejected with a timeout error and the process is killed.

### 🗜 Auto-compact at threshold
- New **`auto_compact_threshold`** config field on `AgentConfig` (default **85**, `0` disables).
- When a chat's context crosses the threshold, the session automatically sends `/compact` to the CLI before the user's next message. The CLI summarizes the conversation (e.g. 48k → 1k tokens) and processes the real message in the compacted context.
- Compaction surfaces as a visible bubble: **"Conversation compacted (48.5k → 1.1k tokens)."**
- Users can also type `/compact` directly as a chat message to trigger compaction on demand.
- Rate-limited to once per 30s to prevent loops.

### 🎛️ UI audit — previously hidden fields now exposed
- **`auto_compact_threshold`** — numeric input in the Execution section of both create and edit forms, shown in the agent-detail view.
- **`wiki_references`** — checkbox list of Wiki Keepers (appears only when at least one keeper exists in the fleet), shown in the agent-detail view.

### 📘 Fleet Orchestrator knowledge refresh
- `agent-fleet-system` skill rewritten to cover: Wiki Keeper (inbox vs watched modes, three bundled skills, scope config), consumer agents (`wiki_references`), chat threading, model picker with aliases + per-task override, auto-compact, extended run-log frontmatter.
- New reference sections on Model Resolution, Shared Subscription Rate Limits, and Error Handling in Chat.
- Four new worked examples: consumer wiki access, per-task cheap-model override, creating a Wiki Keeper, tuning auto-compact threshold.

## Upgrade notes

All changes are backward-compatible. Existing agents auto-pick up the new defaults:
- Missing `auto_compact_threshold` → treated as `85`.
- Missing `wiki_references` → treated as empty (no consumer access).

On next Edit → Save of any existing agent, both fields persist explicitly to the frontmatter.

## Install

**Via BRAT:** `denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet` — update to latest.
**Via npm:** `npm update -g obsidian-agent-fleet`
**Manual:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` below and drop into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/agent-fleet/`.
releases.name: v0.9.1 — error surfacing, auto-compact, UI audit

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## Highlights

### 📚 Wiki Keeper (new)
Turn any folder in your vault into a self-maintaining wiki. Drop sources into an inbox, point at existing note folders as passive watched sources, and a scoped keeper agent ingests them nightly into an interlinked `_topics/` tree with cross-references, citations, and a log. Scales from one whole-vault keeper to many project-scoped instances. See the [Wiki Keeper Guide](https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet/blob/main/WIKI_KEEPER_GUIDE.md).

Key capabilities:
- **Two ingestion modes** — Inbox (destructive, summarize, archive to `YYYY/MM/`) vs Watched (non-destructive, extract durable claims from existing notes)
- **Multi-scope isolation** — run separate keepers per project; no cross-contamination
- **Three bundled skills** — `wiki-ingest` (dual-mode), `wiki-query` (keeper + consumer modes), `wiki-lint` (weekly audit)
- **Consumer agents** — add `wiki_references:` to any agent's config and it can read/contribute to wikis it doesn't own (great for PM agents)
- **Sibling lint task** per keeper with its own schedule
- **Settings UI** — Add / Edit / Delete, human-friendly schedule picker with weekly+day support

### 💬 Chat threading
Inline threads under any assistant reply. Each thread has its own Claude session, context, and stats. Soft-fork seeding replays parent history up to the anchor message; main thread stays completely clean.

### 📊 Terminal-style chat stats strip
Compact one-line display under the composer: model name + context usage bar + percent. No labels (self-explanatory). Cost and quota pills removed.

### 🎯 Focused run output
Run-detail panel now leads with the clean final answer (extracted from the CLI's `result` event). Full assistant transcript collapses behind a ▸ Show full transcript toggle.

### 🎛️ Model-selection redesign
- **Per-task model override** — route a simple nightly summary to Haiku while keeping the agent on Opus
- **New ModelPicker component** — Default / alias (`opus`/`sonnet`/`haiku`/`opusplan`, backend-agnostic) / Custom (for pinned IDs or Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry formats)
- **Single resolution path** — task → agent → settings → CLI default
- **Run logs** record both the requested alias and the concrete resolved model

### 🎨 UI polish
- Thread badge + tool-calls button share one row, matched heights and styling
- User chat bubbles — accent-stroke-only (no fill)
- Archive granularity — `YYYY/MM/` instead of flat `YYYY/`

## Upgrade notes

Existing Wiki Keepers created before 0.9.0 need their tool allowlist updated (adds `Bash(mv *)` so inbox sources actually get moved to the archive instead of copied). **Settings → Wiki Keepers → Edit → Save** on each existing keeper — no fields need to change; the edit helper syncs the allowlist automatically.

## Install

**Via BRAT:** add `denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet` as a beta plugin.
**Via npm:** `npm install -g obsidian-agent-fleet`
**Manual:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` below and drop into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/agent-fleet/`.
releases.name: v0.9.0 — Wiki Keeper, chat threading, run-output focus

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Effort level setting** for agents and tasks — controls Claude's reasoning depth via `--effort` (low, medium, high, max)
- Agent-level effort applies to all runs (tasks, heartbeats, chat)
- Task-level effort overrides the agent's setting per-task
- Full UI support in agent and task create/edit forms
- Default skill docs updated with effort field documentation
releases.name: v0.8.2 — Effort Level for Agents & Tasks

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## Changes

- **Updated default agent & skill** — Fleet Orchestrator and agent-fleet-system skill now reflect the current plugin state:
  - Telegram channels documented as supported (was "coming soon")
  - MCP server management section added (assigning to agents, OAuth authentication flow)
  - Credentials noted as stored in OS keychain (was incorrectly listed as data.json)
  - Cross-platform CLI spawning documented (macOS, Windows, Linux)
  - Removed nonexistent adapter options from config template
  - New examples: Telegram channel setup, MCP server assignment to agents

- **Typing indicator fix** — Streaming dot no longer gets stuck after sending a follow-up message during agent generation. The indicator now correctly reflects whether more turns are pending.

- **Dead code cleanup** — Removed unused `showWorkingIndicator` method

**Note:** Updated defaults only apply to new installations. Existing users who haven't customized the Fleet Orchestrator or agent-fleet-system skill will get the updates automatically on next plugin load.
releases.name: v0.8.1 — Updated Defaults & Typing Indicator Fix

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## What's New

**Cross-platform support** — the plugin now works on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

### Changes
- New `src/utils/platform.ts` module with cross-platform CLI spawning, browser launch, path resolution, and line splitting utilities
- **macOS/Linux:** Login shell spawning preserved (`/bin/zsh -l -c` on macOS, `/bin/bash -l -c` on Linux) so `~/.zshenv` environment variables are available
- **Windows:** Direct CLI spawning (no shell wrapper needed since Windows Obsidian inherits env vars from the system)
- Replaced all `process.env.HOME` with `os.homedir()` (works on all platforms)
- Replaced macOS-only `spawn("open", ...)` with platform-aware browser launch
- Fixed all `.split("\n")` to handle Windows `\r\n` line endings
- Updated CLI installer (`bin/cli.js`) to find Claude CLI on Windows via `where` command
- Refactored MCP server add/remove to use args arrays instead of shell-escaped strings

### Files Modified
- `src/utils/platform.ts` (new)
- `src/services/mcpManager.ts`
- `src/services/executionManager.ts`
- `src/services/chatSession.ts`
- `src/main.ts`
- `src/services/fleetRuntime.ts`
- `src/views/dashboardView.ts`
- `src/fleetRepository.ts`
- `bin/cli.js`
releases.name: v0.8.0 — Cross-Platform Support

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Plugin-owned OAuth 2.1 PKCE flow** — no more CLI delegation; browser opens directly from Obsidian
- **Full MCP spec OAuth discovery** — 401 → resource metadata (RFC 9728) → auth server metadata (RFC 8414)
- **Cross-domain auth** — servers like Todoist (MCP on ai.todoist.net, auth on todoist.com) now work
- **Token persistence** — OAuth tokens read from ~/.claude.json on reload; no re-auth needed
- **Tasks board fix** — only the actual running task shows as "Running", not all tasks for the agent
- **Active agents panel fix** — shows the specific running task or "Heartbeat"
- **Safe auth flow** — MCP servers are never removed without explicit user action
releases.name: v0.7.1 — OAuth PKCE, Cross-Domain Discovery, Task Board Fixes

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## What's changed

**MCP Auth Refactoring** — The plugin no longer runs its own OAuth 2.1 flow (PKCE, dynamic client registration, callback server, token refresh). All OAuth is now delegated to Claude CLI, which owns the full token lifecycle.

### Changes
- **Simplified auth**: Clicking "Authenticate" on an MCP server now runs `claude mcp add` which triggers CLI's native OAuth flow (opens browser, stores token in keychain)
- **Removed API key field**: The Add Server form no longer has an API key input — OAuth-based servers authenticate via CLI, and env vars are still checked as a fallback for tool probing
- **-762 lines**: Removed the entire plugin OAuth implementation (`McpAuthManager` went from 678 lines to 30)
- **Probe tokens**: Plugin extracts Bearer tokens from `claude mcp get` output for its own tool discovery, refreshed every 30 minutes

### Why
The plugin's OAuth flow was fundamentally broken for agent use — it authenticated the plugin but not the CLI. Claude CLI has its own OAuth state machine, so running two parallel auth flows caused "Connected" status in the plugin but "needs authentication" errors during agent runs. Now there's a single source of truth.

### Upgrade notes
- Existing MCP servers that used API keys: re-authenticate via the "Authenticate" button
- No data migration needed — the change is backward compatible
releases.name: v0.7.0 — Delegate MCP OAuth to Claude CLI

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## What's new

### Telegram Image Support
Send photos and image documents to your agents via Telegram. Images are downloaded, saved to the vault, and passed to agents via file path — agents read them with their Read tool, same as the interactive chat view.

### OAuth Fixes
- **Electron compatibility** — all OAuth HTTP calls switched from raw Node `https` to Obsidian's `requestUrl`, fixing silent failures in Electron context that prevented authentication with servers like LaunchDarkly
- **RFC 8414 compliance** — `.well-known` OAuth discovery URLs now include the server path suffix (e.g. `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/mcp/fm`), required for servers with non-root MCP endpoints
- **Header casing** — normalize response headers to lowercase, fixing `www-authenticate` lookup on some platforms
- **Diagnostic errors** — OAuth discovery failures now include which step failed for easier debugging

### Docs
- README updated with v0.6.0 features: secure token storage, MCP server management UI, unified OAuth flow

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet/compare/v0.5.1...v0.6.1
releases.name: v0.6.1 — Telegram Images, OAuth Fixes

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## What's Changed

Comprehensive reliability, security, and quality audit with 26 fixes across P0–P3 priorities.

### Security (P0)
- OAuth CSRF fix: state parameter validation in MCP auth callback
- CLI path validation rejects shell metacharacters

### Reliability (P0/P1)
- Fixed event listener leaks in runtime subscriptions and chat sessions
- Conversation lock GC via generation counter (prevents unbounded memory growth)
- YAML parse crash guard (malformed frontmatter no longer kills plugin load)
- WebSocket handler race fix + 30s connect timeout (Slack)
- Telegram poll cancellation via AbortController (instant shutdown)
- stdin write error handling in chat sessions
- `Promise.allSettled` for MCP server probes (one failure no longer aborts batch)
- Heartbeat dedup prevents duplicate concurrent runs

### Quality (P2/P3)
- Thread context eviction cap (500 entries) and send queue GC (Slack)
- Memory write failures no longer mark successful runs as failed
- Conversation locks drain before teardown in stop()
- Reconnect timer race guard
- Removed unsupported "discord" channel type
- Accessibility: `role="button"`, `tabindex`, keyboard handlers on sidebar; `aria-label` on copy buttons
- `suppressVaultEvents` timer properly stored and cleared on unload
- Production build now minified (28% smaller bundle)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/denberek/obsidian-agent-fleet/compare/v0.5.0...v0.5.1
releases.name: v0.5.1 — Reliability & Quality Improvements

releases.author: denberek
releases.description:
## Telegram Adapter

Chat with your agents from Telegram — simpler setup than Slack, zero dependencies.

- **30-second setup** — create a bot via @BotFather, paste the token, create a channel
- **Long-poll** via HTTPS (no WebSocket, no SDK, works behind any firewall)
- **Typing indicator** — `typing...` dots while the agent works, auto-refreshed every 4.5s
- **Inline keyboard agent picker** — `/agents` command shows interactive buttons to switch agents
- **Slash command autocomplete** — commands registered automatically on adapter start
- **Group chat support** — add the bot to Telegram groups (disable privacy mode for full access)
- **Forum topics** — enable Threaded Mode in BotFather for topic-based conversations
- **4096-char message splitting** — long replies chunked at paragraph boundaries

## Interactive /agents Command (Slack + Telegram)

The `/agents` command now returns **clickable buttons** instead of a plain text list on both platforms:
- **Slack** — Block Kit buttons with checkmark on the active agent
- **Telegram** — inline keyboard with one button per agent
- Clicking a button switches the thread's agent binding immediately
- The original message updates to show the current selection

## Agent Name in Replies

When multiple agents are configured on a channel, every reply is prefixed with `*[agent-name]*` so you always know which agent responded. Works on both Slack and Telegram.

## Slack Fixes

- **`app_mention` text stripping** — bot mention (`<@BOT_ID>`) is now stripped from the message before processing, so @mentions in channels work correctly
- **Channel agent binding** — `/agents` button clicks now apply to all future messages from the user in that channel (fixed conversation ID mismatch)
- **Chat copy button** — preserved during streaming (was being destroyed by `setText` on every text chunk)

## Other Improvements

- **Chat session restore** — sessions load correctly after Obsidian reload (fixed `hasChildNodes` guard)
- **Type augmentation** — `WorkspaceLeaf.updateHeader()` properly declared
- **Clean typecheck** — zero errors from our code
- **Auto-updating defaults** — fleet-orchestrator and agent-fleet-system skill update automatically on plugin load without overwriting user customizations

## Install / Update

```bash
npm install -g obsidian-agent-fleet
# or
npm update -g obsidian-agent-fleet
```

Plugin files are automatically copied to all detected Obsidian vaults via the postinstall hook.

### Telegram Quick Start

1. Message @BotFather → `/newbot` → copy the token
2. Obsidian → Settings → Agent Fleet → Channel Credentials → Type: Telegram → paste token
3. Dashboard → Channels → + New Channel → Type: Telegram
4. Message the bot in Telegram
releases.name: v0.5.0 — Telegram, Interactive Agent Picker, Multi-Transport Channels
